revenue: $7.08 billion, up 1.8% YoY compared to $6.95 billion in Q2 2024
net_income: $1.96 billion, up 21.4% YoY compared to $1.61 billion in Q2 2024
key_metrics: Product sales of $7.05 billion, up from $6.91 billion YoY
Diluted EPS of $1.56, up from $1.29 YoY
Cash and cash equivalents of $5.14 billion, down from $9.99 billion at Dec 31, 2024
R&D expenses of $1.49 billion, up from $1.35 billion YoY
qoq_changes: Implied Q2 2025 revenue of $7.08 billion represents sequential growth from implied Q1 2025 revenue of $6.67 billion (based on $13.75 billion six-month total)
Continued reduction in cash and cash equivalents balance since year-end 2024
new_risks: Potential impacts from the newly cited 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' alongside the Inflation Reduction Act
Changes in U.S. trade policies, specifically including new tariffs
management_tone: Cautiously optimistic; while the company delivered solid YoY earnings and revenue growth, forward-looking statements heavily emphasize emerging legislative and tariff-related headwinds.
analyst_note: Gilead demonstrated strong bottom-line leverage in Q2 2025, growing net income 21.4% on modest 1.8% revenue growth. However, a significant reduction in cash reserves and newly flagged macro risks regarding U.S. tariffs and healthcare legislation warrant close monitoring.
